item.title: Iran offers new proposal for peace talks with the U.S.
item.description: Iran says it's offered a new proposal for talks with the U.S. to end the war, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: Book excerpt: "The New Tourist" by Paige McClanahan
item.description: Journalist Paige McClanahan writes about how tourism shapes societies and individuals, and about the need to redefine the meaning of "tourist" in today's shrinking world.

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down as soon as Saturday
item.description: Negotiations over a $500 million dollar government aid package for Spirit stalled after bondholders balked at the terms.

item.title: Trump says he's hiking tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25%
item.description: President Trump said the European Union "is not complying with our fully agreed to trade deal."

item.title: Remains of teen sailor who helped rescue downed WWII airmen identified
item.description: U.S. Navy Aviation Radioman 2nd Class Robert L. Cyr Jr. enlisted at 17 and flew patrols in the Pacific before his death at 19.

item.title: Comey faces charges over "86 47" post. How far will the case get?
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey was charged with two counts arising out a now-deleted image he shared on Instagram that showed seashells arranged to read "86 47."

item.title: Nebraska becomes first U.S. state to enact Medicaid work requirements
item.description: The "big beautiful bill" requires U.S. states to add work requirements to Medicaid by January 2027. Experts warn millions could lose health coverage.

item.title: Gas prices get worse as Iran war enters second month
item.description: The Iran war has reached the 60-day mark, as data shows gas prices setting a concerning record. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Kelly O'Grady report.

item.title: Superdry co-founder James Holder convicted of rape in England
item.description: James Holder, 54, was found guilty by a jury in Gloucester Crown Court of one count of rape for the May 2022 assault.

item.title: $150,000 CD vs. $150,000 high-yield savings vs. $150,000 money market account: Which option earns more now?
item.description: A $150,000 deposit can generate big interest, but only if it's in the right account. Here's which earns more now.

item.title: Gold House reveals new Gold100 list
item.description: Gold House revealed its 2026 Gold100 list honoring Asian Pacific culture. Co-founder and CEO Bing Chen joins CBS News with more.

item.title: Book excerpt: "A Course Called Home" by Tom Coyne
item.description: The bestselling author and editor of The Golfer's Journal teed up for a challenge – taking over operations of a failing nine-hole community golf course in New York's Catskill Mountains – and writes of the tribulations that were par for the course.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 3)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: ICE reports 18th detainee death in 2026, putting agency on track for new record
item.description: ICE reported the 18th death of an individual in its custody so far this year, putting the agency on track to record a new all-time high in detainee deaths.

item.title: Video captures massive explosion as NYPD officers approach home, 1 dead
item.description: A body believed to be that of the suspect was recovered after an explosion triggered a five-alarm fire at a home in Queens early Thursday morning.

item.title: Lizzo: "There's a long list" of things the public gets wrong about me
item.description: The pop star sat down with Gayle King for an exclusive interview airing Monday on "CBS Mornings."

item.title: Lizzo explains what the public gets wrong about her: "I'm just shining a light"
item.description: Lizzo sits down with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King to discuss what the public misunderstands about her.

item.title: Man facing ICE deportation to the Congo: "I feel like a person who has no value"
item.description: Jose Yugar-Cruz was granted a court order preventing his deportation to his home country, but the Trump administration is set to send him to the Congo.

item.title: Bill Nye on meeting the Artemis II crew and his take on the historic moon mission
item.description: Bill Nye The Science Guy, the chief ambassador of The Planetary Society, joins CBS News 24/7 Mornings with more after meeting the Artemis II crew in person after their successful mission to the moon.

item.title: Confrontation between officers and alleged shooter at D.C. gala lasted 7 seconds
item.description: Seven seconds passed between when the alleged gunman at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner — carrying a shotgun initially concealed by a jacket — first encountered federal law enforcement and when he was subdued, sources told CBS News.

item.title: How the 76-day partial government shutdown ended
item.description: The 76-day partial government shutdown, which impacted funding for the Department of Homeland Security, has ended. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Iran sends U.S. revised proposal for talks to end war, Pakistani officials say
item.description: Iran delivered a revised response to the latest U.S. amendments on an agreement to end the war, Pakistan officials tell CBS News. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: 2 employees killed in Kentucky bank robbery, person of interest in custody
item.description: Kentucky State Police said a man went to a U.S. Bank in Brea, Kentucky, and shot and killed a man and a woman, both employees at the bank.

item.title: Artemis II crew surprises 5-year-old boy and answers true or false space questions
item.description: The Artemis II crew surprised a 5-year-old boy space enthusiast and answered "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King's true or false questions about burping in space and whether you become taller in space. The crew appeared for a CBS News town hall "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes."

item.title: Bill Nye demonstrates experiments that break down Artemis II mission
item.description: Bill Nye The Science Guy, the chief ambassador of The Planetary Society, joins "CBS Mornings" to demonstrate some experiments that break down the Artemis II mission.

item.title: Artemis astronauts talk "bird bath" showers, space exploration dream and more
item.description: Artemis astronauts answered questions from students on the advice they would give to aspiring astronauts, their dream for space exploration, and also detailed the toilet malfunction and what showering in space was like.

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

item.title: Ron Howard, Artemis astronauts joke about bathrooms while recalling "Apollo 13" film's impact
item.description: Ron Howard joined "CBS Mornings" to talk about directing his 11995 film, "Apollo 13," and the duality of watching the Artemis II astronauts' liftoff in April. The crew also shared how Howard's film impacted them, and Howard recalled a story where he told Kevin Bacon, "You get 10 extra close-ups if you keep your mouth shut!"

item.title: Artemis astronauts describe "spectacular" moon mission, eating peanut M&M's after splashdown
item.description: Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a town hall, "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes." They described the moments before liftoff and splashdown, and the funny moment just after coming back to Earth that prompted "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King to say, "astronauts, they're just like us."

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly dumping wife's body in zoo incinerator
item.description: Police arrested a man for allegedly incinerating his dead wife at the zoo where he worked, officials said, following the discovery of human remains.
